---
title: "LRP75: ITT analysis of taught receptive vocabulary (block 1)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
tau_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRP75 is the **receptive** companion to LRP74. The outcome is the Block 1
taught-words *receptive* test (`b1retau`, symbol `TR`): a bespoke 24-item test of
the same explicitly-taught words, but assessed receptively (select the picture
matching the target word). The estimand is again the randomised-window
(t1 $\rightarrow$ t2) effect of group assignment $G$.

::: {.callout-important title="Expected asymmetry: expressive ≠ receptive"}

The published trial found gains on directly-taught *expressive* vocabulary
"though not on the equivalent receptive vocabulary measure" (Burgoyne et al.,
2012). The authors attribute this expressive/receptive asymmetry to the teaching
emphasis on *using* the words and to the differing task demands of the two tests.
The expectation here is therefore a **weaker / near-null** effect than LRP74 —
and that asymmetry is itself the finding. LRP74 and LRP75 are reported as a pair;
neither should be collapsed into a single "taught vocabulary" effect.

:::

## Model

The linear predictor for child $i$ is

$$
\eta_i = \alpha
       + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_{\text{own}}\, \text{logit}_*(\mathrm{TR}_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N_{\mathrm{TR}})
       + \gamma_{R}\, \text{logit}_*(R_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N_R),
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood on $\mathrm{TR}_{\text{post},i}$ and
$N_{\mathrm{TR}} = 24$. As in LRP74, the outcome is conditioned on its own
taught-vocabulary baseline plus the matched standardised baseline — here the
standardised *receptive*-vocabulary test $R$ (ROWPVT) — rather than all eight ITT
baselines. HSGPs off by default. Priors follow `priors.py`.

::: {.callout-note title="Denominator"}

$N_{\mathrm{TR}} = 24$ is documented (six words of each of four types; Burgoyne et
al., 2012, Table 3).

:::

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=40%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=40%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=40%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Treatment-effect summary

::: {.callout-important title="Sign convention: positive τ = intervention benefit"}

$\tau$ is the coefficient on $G = 2 - \text{group}$, so $G = 1$ is the
**immediate-intervention** arm and $G = 0$ the waiting-control arm. So a
**positive $\tau$ means the intervention raised taught receptive vocabulary**, and
the probability of benefit is $P(\tau > 0) = \texttt{prob\_tau\_pos}$ (suite-wide
convention; see METHODS.md). The expectation here is a $\tau$ *closer to zero* than
LRP74 — the expressive/receptive asymmetry.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary
```

- `tau_logit_*`: posterior mean and equal-tailed interval of $\tau$ on the logit scale ($\tau > 0$ ⇒ benefit).
- `tau_prob_*`: implied marginal effect on the probability scale; the intervention benefit in words is $\texttt{tau\_prob\_mean} \times 24$.
- `prob_tau_pos`: $P(\tau > 0)$; the probability the intervention helped.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
